methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $185M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Space & Aerospace. Range: $274M - $1.3B.

data.narrative:
Heart Aerospace, a Series B space and aerospace company that has raised $185M to date, is valued herein using two applicable triangulation methods against a comparable universe of 3 private peers in the same sector and stage (Aalyria, Hubble Network, Sift) and 5 public sector benchmarks (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL).

Stage-progression methodology applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier to the $185M total raised as a proxy valuation floor, yielding $352M. Peer-median valuation anchors to the median across three same-stage, same-sector private companies at $400M, with the comparable range spanning $274M (Sift) to $1.3B (Aalyria). Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiples (sector median 49.8x) is not applicable absent disclosed revenue data. The two applicable methods triangulate to a range of $299M to $460M, with a median implied valuation of $376M.

Key uncertainties center on the wide dispersion within the peer set—the $1.3B Aalyria outlier reflects heterogeneous technology risk and customer capture profiles within aerospace. The stage-progression method carries model risk inherent to historical multiplier assumptions. Revenue disclosure or customer concentration metrics would sharpen public-comp applicability and reduce valuation bandwidth. The analysis supports a valuation range of approximately $350M–$400M as a central estimate, with $299M–$460M as the broader defensible range.
